predictions.text: By 2026-06-15, the BLS CPI report for May 2026 will show headline CPI inflation (CPI-U, U.S. city average) at or above 3.6% year-over-year.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the BLS CPI news release covering May 2026 reports CPI-U (All items) 12-month percent change >= 3.6%. Otherwise NO.
evidence: March CPI rose 0.9% m/m (3.3% y/y), with gasoline/energy surge tied to Iran conflict, complicating disinflation.
Real average hourly earnings fell 0.6% in March, consistent with inflation pressure outpacing wages.
monitoring_signals: Weekly AAA gasoline price trend and EIA product supplied for gasoline/jet fuel
Cleveland Fed Inflation Nowcast for headline CPI
Brent/WTI and refined-product crack spreads into May
predictions.rationale: Energy-driven pass-through and elevated monthly prints make a modest upward drift in y/y plausible, but base effects still limit odds of a large move.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, at least one major European hub airport (LHR, CDG, FRA, AMS, MAD, FCO, or DUB) will publicly confirm jet-fuel supply constraints that lead to either (a) fuel uplift limits for airlines or (b) cancellation of at least 50 departing flights within a 72-hour window, with the constraint explicitly linked to Middle East conflict/shipping disruption.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an airport operator statement, regulator notice, or major airline statement names one of the listed airports and confirms jet-fuel supply constraints plus (a) explicit uplift limits OR (b) >=50 departures canceled in 72 hours, and explicitly links the constraint to Middle East conflict/shipping disruption. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Europe faces potential jet-fuel crunch within weeks, highlighting war-driven energy shocks’ spillovers to transport.
Hormuz security risks keep oil and jet-fuel supply fragile.
monitoring_signals: IATA/ACI Europe advisories mentioning fuel supply
Airport operations notices (NOTAMs, airport press offices) referencing fuel constraints
European jet fuel spot prices and inventories (ARA region), tanker rates and insurance costs
predictions.rationale: A shortage is being discussed publicly, but translating into confirmed operational rationing/cancellation at a major hub requires a sharper logistics break.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-31, The Senior Citizens League (TSCL) or a comparably prominent U.S. public estimator will publish an estimate for the 2027 Social Security COLA of at least 3.8%.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if TSCL (preferred) or another widely cited public COLA estimator posts a 2027 COLA estimate >= 3.8% on its website or in a press release by the deadline. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Higher gasoline prices pushed up early estimates for 2027 Social Security COLA, implying larger federal outlays and benefit adjustments.
March CPI showed a sharp monthly increase consistent with near-term inflation pressure.
monitoring_signals: CPI-W trajectory for Jul–Sep 2026 (key COLA reference months)
Gasoline and utility components within CPI-W
TSCL monthly COLA estimate updates
predictions.rationale: With energy volatile, a high-3% estimate is plausible, though sustained cooling in CPI-W would pull estimates back down.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-10, TSMC will report (via its monthly revenue press releases and quarterly aggregation) that Q2 2026 revenue increased by at least 25% year-over-year versus Q2 2025.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the sum of TSMC’s April+May+June 2026 monthly revenue (as reported by TSMC) is >= 1.25x the sum of April+May+June 2025 monthly revenue. Otherwise NO.
evidence: TSMC reported record revenue up 35% y/y, reflecting sustained AI chip demand.
monitoring_signals: TSMC monthly revenue releases for Apr/May/Jun 2026
Foundry pricing/utilization commentary from peers (Samsung, GlobalFoundries)
AI server shipment and HBM supply-chain indicators
predictions.rationale: Recent 35% y/y growth suggests strong momentum; 25% for Q2 is achievable unless a demand or supply shock hits late quarter.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-31, at least one of Walmart, Target, Home Depot, or Lowe’s will explicitly cite higher gasoline/energy prices as a material headwind to U.S. discretionary consumer demand during an earnings call or in an earnings press release (verbatim mention of gasoline/fuel as a demand headwind).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an earnings call transcript or earnings release from any listed retailer contains an explicit statement that higher gasoline/fuel/energy prices are (or were) a headwind to consumer spending/demand. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Real average hourly earnings fell 0.6% in March, indicating inflation outpaced wages and squeezed purchasing power.
Gasoline/energy surge contributed materially to recent CPI increases.
monitoring_signals: Retailer earnings calendars and prepared remarks
Gasoline price levels vs prior year and consumer sentiment readings
Credit card spending trackers by category (gas vs discretionary)
predictions.rationale: When fuel spikes and real earnings fall, large retailers commonly highlight gas as a squeeze on discretionary budgets.

predictions.text: By 2026-11-30, at least two U.S. bank regulators (Federal Reserve, OCC, FDIC, or FFIEC) will issue a public guidance document (statement, bulletin, supervisory letter, or equivalent) that explicitly addresses governance/model risk for generative AI use in banking (must include the phrase 'generative AI' or 'GenAI').
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if by the deadline two or more of: Fed, OCC, FDIC, FFIEC publish public guidance explicitly containing 'generative AI' or 'GenAI' and addressing governance/model risk/controls for banks. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Anthropic’s ‘Mythos’ prompted Powell/Bessent briefings with major banks on cyber/operational risk, signaling regulators’ growing focus on frontier-model threats.
monitoring_signals: FFIEC press releases and joint statements
OCC/Fed/FDIC supervisory bulletin pages
Conference speeches hinting at upcoming supervisory expectations on GenAI
predictions.rationale: Regulatory attention is rising, but agencies may prefer nonbinding speeches over formal guidance within this timeframe.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, CoreWeave will publicly file an IPO registration statement (S-1 or F-1) on SEC EDGAR for a U.S. listing (confidential submissions do not count).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an S-1 or F-1 for CoreWeave appears publicly on SEC EDGAR by the deadline. Otherwise NO.
evidence: CoreWeave shares jumped on a deal to power Anthropic’s Claude, highlighting surging demand for GPU cloud capacity and AI infrastructure monetization.
monitoring_signals: CoreWeave hiring for SEC reporting/IR roles
Underwriter rumors and media reports of IPO prep
Quarterly growth signals from AI infrastructure contracts
predictions.rationale: Strong demand could support an IPO, but market windows and financing alternatives (private rounds) may delay a public filing.

predictions.text: By 2026-10-31, France’s central government digital authority (DINUM or an equivalent official body) will publish a national-level Windows-to-Linux migration roadmap that includes (a) at least one dated milestone in 2027 or earlier and (b) names at least three ministries/agencies participating.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official French government publication (DINUM or equivalent) releases a roadmap/plan for replacing Windows with Linux that contains at least one milestone date in 2027 or earlier and explicitly names >=3 ministries/agencies. Otherwise NO.
evidence: France plans to replace Windows with Linux to reduce dependence on U.S. tech vendors, accelerating ‘digital sovereignty’ procurement across governments.
monitoring_signals: DINUM publications and procurement framework announcements
French Parliament questions/ministry responses on software sovereignty
Large French public-sector IT tenders specifying Linux requirements
predictions.rationale: Sovereignty push is real, but converting a headline into a detailed, dated, multi-ministry roadmap may take longer than several months.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, at least one additional EU national government (Germany, Italy, Spain, Netherlands, Belgium, or Austria) will announce a central-government initiative to migrate a significant share of desktops to Linux or an open-source OS, explicitly citing digital sovereignty or reduced dependence on U.S. vendors.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official government press release/ministerial statement (not municipal) from any listed country announces a central-government Linux/open-source OS migration initiative and explicitly cites sovereignty/vendor-dependence as motivation. Otherwise NO.
evidence: France’s move to ditch Windows for Linux reflects rising ‘digital sovereignty’ procurement across governments.
monitoring_signals: EU member state digital-ministry announcements and national procurement frameworks
Parliamentary committees on cybersecurity/sovereignty referencing OS choices
Major open-source vendors reporting new national contracts
predictions.rationale: France can catalyze imitation, but national-level OS migrations are politically and operationally heavy, limiting near-term follow-through.
